channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: The CFTC has sparked a potential revolution on Wall Street. Exchange stocks are dropping
item.description: Investors are worried about increased competition for the traditional exchanges that have long dominated on Wall Street.

item.title: Rubio says U.S. is in talks with Iran over nuclear program as senators press for war endgame
item.description: Rubio says Iran nuclear talks may advance soon as senators press Trump on war strategy, Strait of Hormuz, Cuba and Venezuela policy.

item.title: Trump signs AI executive order asking companies to give government early access to models
item.description: AI developers, on a voluntary basis, are asked to collaborate with the government and provide early access to frontier models.

item.title: Trump names housing chief Bill Pulte acting intelligence director, replacing Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: Pulte has alleged wrongdoing related to mortgages against Trump's foes, Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook and New York Attorney General Letitia James.

item.title: Job openings in April surged to 7.6 million, the highest in nearly two years
item.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that available employment hit 7.6 million for the month, a surge of 731,000 from the prior month.

item.title: Here are JPMorgan's favorite stocks heading into June
item.description: The Wall Street investment bank shares the top ideas from its own equity analysts, from technology to industrials to transportation.

item.title: Iowa Democratic Senate primary could shape Democrats' path to Senate majority
item.description: Iowa's Senate primary between state Rep. Josh Turek and state Sen. Zach Wahls could be critical as Democrats look to take back power in Congress.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: Coherent, Victoria's Secret, Marvell, HPE, Generac, Intuit & more
item.description: Here are the companies making headlines in midday trading.

item.title: Marvell stock soars 20% as Nvidia's Huang says it could be the next trillion-dollar company
item.description: Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, hailed this chip designer as the next trillion-dollar firm, sending its shares up sharply in early market trading.

item.title: Nvidia's new PC chips represent CEO Huang's bid to win at every layer of AI stack
item.description: Nvidia's announced entry into the PC chip market sent shares of AMD, Intel and Qualcomm lower as Wall Street recognized the threat.

item.title: Audemars Piguet's watch prices remain stable after controversial Swatch collab
item.description: The Royal Pop collection of brightly colored watches on lanyards is part of AP's longer-term strategy of attracting the next generation of collectors.

item.title: The top 10 highest-paying jobs for teens in the U.S.—No. 1 isn't retail or food service
item.description: Babysitting may not be your ultimate career path, but having a job as a teenager can build transferable skills for young workers, according to experts.

item.title: Polymarket closes its first block trade as prediction markets push for Wall Street adoption
item.description: Prediction market platforms have increasingly looked to institutional traders, not individual ones, as the next venue for growth.

item.title: Anthropic expands Mythos to 150 additional organizations in more than 15 countries
item.description: Anthropic initially released Project Glasswing to about 50 partners in April to test the model for cybersecurity flaws.

item.title: Microsoft and Google are late to AI coding, but 'absolutely critical' they compete for growth
item.description: Coding tools are becoming an increasingly big target for Google and Microsoft as they try to catch up to Anthropic and OpenAI.

item.title: HPE surges 15% after blowout earnings, pacing for one of its best days ever
item.description: CEO Antonio Neri told CNBC that the company is "uniquely positioned" to capture the disruption brought about by artificial intelligence.

item.title: How Jim Cramer would approach Palo Alto's stock heading into earnings
item.description: The Investing Club holds its "Morning Meeting" every weekday at 10:20 a.m. ET.

item.title: Commodities regulator alleges Winklevosses' Gemini crypto exchange was politically targeted
item.description: CFTC chair Michael Selig said the agency's enforcement action against Gemini was part of a broader campaign against the crypto industry under President Biden.

item.title: There's a compelling opportunity in this ridesharing name
item.description: Nishant Pant walks CNBC Pro subscribers through this bull call spread.

item.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80 billion from stock sales to fund AI build-out
item.description: Alphabet said it plans to sell $80 billion in stock, including through a $10 billion investment by Berkshire Hathaway.

item.title: Victoria's Secret shares spike 40% after big earnings beat, raised sales outlook
item.description: Victoria's Secret has been in the midst of a turnaround under CEO Hillary Super, who said the company is winning over young shoppers across all incomes.

item.title: Student loans are easier than ever to get discharged in bankruptcy. You should still try these 5 alternatives first
item.description: Biden-era guidance streamlined the bankruptcy process for student loan borrowers. But that doesn't make it a no-brainer.

item.title: Homeowners insurance premiums have soared in recent years. How to reduce your costs
item.description: Insurance premiums have soared for many homeowners in recent years, but there are steps policyholders can take to reduce them, experts said.

item.title: Software stocks just passed a big milestone
item.description: Software stocks are making a comeback from their artificial intelligence-driven sell-off earlier this year.

item.title: This AI server name is surging on big earnings beat. Loop Capital sees more upside ahead
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise, which posted blowout earnings on Monday, is likely to see its shares rise as it benefits from an AI boom, per Loop Capital.

item.title: NBA star Stephen Curry partners with Chinese sportswear giant Li-Ning
item.description: Stephen Curry has entered into a partnership with Li-Ning, which would involve product development as well as sports culture initiatives.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway invests extra $10 billion in Alphabet, deepening bet on AI
item.description: The deal builds on a stake Berkshire has steadily expanded since late 2025, making Alphabet one of its biggest recent bets.

item.title: Trump tells CNBC: 'I don't care' if Iran negotiations are over
item.description: President Trump spoke with CNBC's Eamon Javers about the latest with the Iran war in an interview Monday.

item.title: Nuclear weapons could soon be hosted in more NATO nations — what it means for defense stocks
item.description: The U.S. is reportedly exploring plans to expand its nuclear weapons-hosting arrangements to include more NATO countries in Europe.

item.title: Can markets suffer from too much of a good thing?
item.description: The market’s extraordinary run to new highs over the past two months has been carried by an array of upbeat fundamental factors and favorable macro conditions.
